channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: DHL cargo plane crashes and skids into a house in Lithuania, killing a Spanish crew member
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — A DHL cargo plane crashed on approach to an airport in Lithuania's capital and skidded into a house Monday morning, killing a Spanish crew member but not harming anyone on the ground. The cause is under investigation.
item.dc:creator: SIARHEI SATSIUK, VANESSA GERA and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel launches new airstrikes on Lebanon as leaders draw closer to a ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel's military launched airstrikes across Lebanon on Monday, unleashing explosions throughout the country and killing at least 31 while Israeli leaders appeared to be closing in on a negotiated ceasefire with the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, JOE FEDERMAN and SAM METZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Carrefour's cold shoulder for South American beef sparks a backlash from Brazil
item.description: Supermarket giant Carrefour’s support for French farmers’ protests against a trade agreement between the European Union and the South American bloc Mercosur has sparked a strong reaction in Brazil.
item.dc:creator: Fabiano Maisonnave, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A far-right populist shocks Romania by heading into the presidential runoff
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romania is reeling after a little-known, far-right populist secured the first round in the presidential election, beating the incumbent prime minister. Calin Georgescu, who ran independently, will face reformist Elena Lasconi in a Dec. 8.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bah, humbug! Vandal smashes Ebenezer Scrooge's tombstone used in 'A Christmas Carol' movie
item.description: If life imitates art, a vandal in the English countryside may be haunted by The Ghost of Christmas Yet to Come.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Scuffles in Serbian parliament as deadly station collapse sparks anger at the government
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Scuffles and fistfights broke out between ruling party and opposition lawmakers in Serbia’s parliament on Monday, weeks after a deadly rail station roof collapse that ignited tensions in the Balkan state.
item.dc:creator: DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Philippine showdown: President says he'll fight vice president's plot to have him killed
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on Monday described a public threat by the vice president to have him killed by an assassin as a criminal plot and vowed to fight it, in a looming showdown between the country’s two top leaders.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel 'moving forward' on possible Hezbollah cease-fire, official says
item.description: The Israeli Security Cabinet will convene Tuesday to discuss a possible cease-fire agreement with Hezbollah terrorists in Lebanon, Ambassador Danny Danon said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia's newly elected parliament opens its first session with opposition boycotting
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — The newly elected Georgian parliament opened its inaugural session Monday as opposition lawmakers and the president boycotted it and thousands of protesters, watched by riot police, rallied outside and accused the ruling party of rigging the vote under Russian influence.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia reportedly captures a Briton fighting for Ukraine as Russian troops advance
item.description: Russia's military captured a British national fighting with Ukrainian troops who have occupied part of Russia's Kursk region, according to reports Monday, as Moscow began daylight drone attacks on civilian areas of Ukraine and its ground forces accelerated gains along parts of the front line.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: G7 foreign ministers meet in Italy with hopes of progress toward Mideast ceasefire
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — Foreign ministers from the world’s leading industrialized nations met Monday as signs emerged of progress on a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah and a certain pressure to advance diplomatic efforts in both the Mideast and Ukraine wars ahead of the new U.S.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Britain on alert after 2nd major storm of the season batters country, leaving at least 2 dead
item.description: Britain remains on high alert after the second major storm of the season battered the country over the weekend, leaving at least two people dead and disrupting road and rail travel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Namibia may elect its first-ever female president in elections this week
item.description: OSHAKATI, Namibia (AP) — Namibia's Vice President Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah could become the country’s first female president if she wins the presidential election Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ELIASER NDEYANALE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel moves toward ceasefire deal with Hezbollah: reports
item.description: Israel is reportedly moving toward a ceasefire agreement with Hezbollah in Lebanon after nearly a year of fighting escalated into an all-out war
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah fires barrage of rockets into Israel after IDF targets Hezbollah command centers in Beirut
item.description: Hezbollah on Sunday fired missiles and other projectiles into Israel in retaliation for to deadly IDF strikes on the militant group’s command centers in Beirut.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Far-right populist surprises in Romanian presidential election appearing set to enter runoff
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — A little-known, far-right populist took the lead in Romania’s presidential election Sunday, electoral data showed, and will likely face leftist Prime Minister Marcel Ciolacu in a runoff in two weeks, an outcome that rocked the country's political landscape.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Intelligence Committee will ask Gabbard 'lots of questions,' Lankford says
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.) said Sunday that the Senate Intelligence Committee will ask President-elect Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, former Democratic Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), “lots of questions.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Heavy rains in Bolivia send mud crashing into the capital, leaving 1 missing and destroying homes
item.description: LA PAZ, Bolivia (AP) — A landslide caused by heavy rains after a prolonged drought in La Paz, the capital of Bolivia, flooded dozens of homes early Sunday, local authorities said. It swept away a young girl, spurred evacuations and left parts of the city without electricity.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.N. reaches agreement on $300 billion climate deal for developing countries
item.description: The United Nations reached a $300 billion climate agreement for developing countries as they grapple with the significant effects of climate change. Delegates agreed Saturday to the billion-dollar pledge after “weeks of intense negotiations,” the U.N. said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Arrest warrants for Israeli leaders amplify Democratic rupture
item.description: The International Criminal Court’s (ICC) decision this week to indict Israeli leaders on charges of war crimes in Gaza has reignited the clash among Democrats over Israel's handling of the war with Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from Israel-Hamas war passes 44,000, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 13-month-old war between Israel and Hamas has surpassed 44,000, local health officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, WGN 9
